decisions.name: Parse Tree Representation
decisions.rationale: ANTLR4 automatically generates a concrete syntax tree (CST); we use built-in visitor/listener patterns to traverse it.

decisions.name: Tree Traversal Pattern
decisions.rationale: Visitor allows returning values from each rule, which is essential for AST construction in Phase 2. Listeners are fire-and-forget.

typeExpr.literalValue: "Permissive token sequences for ACHIEVE, CREATE, LOG, etc."
"Child nodes MUST appear in the same order as their source declarations"

constraints.text: Parse tree MUST contain a node for every GASD construct in the source
Tree traversal MUST preserve source declaration order
Parser API MUST be accessible as a public module

decisions.name: Validation Architecture
decisions.rationale: Each validation pass focuses on one concern: name uniqueness, required sections, reference resolution, location enrichment. Passes can be composed and extended.

decisions.name: Error Collection Strategy
decisions.rationale: Users benefit from seeing all validation errors at once rather than fixing one error at a time.

steps.target: "Collect all TYPE names into a set"
"Verify that no duplicate TYPE names exist"
"Collect all COMPONENT names into a set"
"Verify that no duplicate COMPONENT names exist"
"Collect all FLOW names into a set"
"Verify that no duplicate FLOW names exist"
"Collect all STRATEGY names into a set"
"Verify that no duplicate STRATEGY names exist"
"Collect all DECISION names into a set"
"Verify that no duplicate DECISION names exist"
"All names are unique within their construct kind"

subSteps.target: RETURN "List of SemanticError for each duplicate"

steps.target: "Check that at least one TYPE, COMPONENT, or FLOW exists"
"Check that every COMPONENT has an INTERFACE block"
"Check that every DECISION has a CHOSEN field"
"Check that every FLOW has at least one step"

subSteps.target: RETURN "List of SemanticError for missing sections"

steps.target: "Build symbol table of all declared names"
"Resolve FLOW parameter types to known TYPEs"
"Resolve FLOW return types to known TYPEs"
"Resolve TYPE field types to primitives or declared TYPEs"
"Resolve FLOW VALIDATE statements' AS TYPE binding"
"All references resolve (though unresolved names are reported as WARNINGS to support external symbols)"
"Verify every SemanticError has line and column"
"Verify every SemanticError has a descriptive message"
"Verify error context points to the offending construct name"

constraints.text: Validation MUST be non-halting: collect all errors before reporting
Every error MUST include line, column, and context
Reference resolution MUST cover all cross-construct links
Required sections validation MUST check CONTEXT and TARGET at minimum

decisions.name: Lexer Architecture
decisions.rationale: Indentation handling requires custom lexer logic; separating lexer and parser keeps concerns clean.

decisions.name: Indentation Strategy
decisions.chosen: Custom Lexer with INDENT/DEDENT Token Emission
decisions.rationale: GASD uses Python-style significant whitespace. A custom lexer override tracks indentation depth and emits synthetic INDENT/DEDENT tokens on the token stream.

decisions.name: Comment Handling
decisions.rationale: Single-line (//) and block (/* */) comments are skipped. Documentation comments (///) are sent to a hidden channel for downstream doc generation.

typeExpr.literalValue: "Keywords used as identifiers in non-ambiguous positions"
"Emitted at end of each logical line"

"section ::= context_dir | namespace_stmt | import_stmt | decision_blk | type_def | component_def | flow_def | strategy_def | constraint_stmt | invariant_stmt | match_expr | question_stmt | approve_blk | todo_stmt | review_stmt"
"DECISION STRING_LITERAL annotations? ':' INDENT CHOSEN ':' STRING_LITERAL (RATIONALE ':' STRING_LITERAL)? (ALTERNATIVES ':' list_literal)? (AFFECTS ':' list_literal) DEDENT"
"soft_id | literal_type | 'List' '<' type_expr '>' | 'Map' '<' type_expr ',' type_expr '>' | 'Enum' '(' type_expr (',' type_expr)* ')' | 'Optional' '<' type_expr '>'"

"COMPONENT soft_id annotations? ':' INDENT (PATTERN ':' value)? (DEPENDENCIES ':' list_literal)? (RESOURCES ':' (INDENT bullet_item+ DEDENT | list_literal))? INTERFACE ':' INDENT (method_sig | field_def)+ DEDENT DEDENT"
"Permissive sequences of tokens supporting natural language descriptions in ACHIEVE, CREATE, RETURN, LOG, etc. VALIDATE MUST use explicit binding: VALIDATE expr AS TYPE.typePath."
"ENSURE expr [OTHERWISE action] | MATCH expr | IF expr ... ELSE"
"STRATEGY IDENTIFIER annotations? ':' INDENT ALGORITHM ':' STRING_LITERAL (PRECONDITION ':' expr)? (COMPLEXITY ':' STRING_LITERAL)? INPUT ':' param_list OUTPUT ':' type_expr DEDENT"
"APPROVE STRING_LITERAL ':' INDENT STATUS ':' (APPROVED | REJECTED) BY ':' STRING_LITERAL DATE ':' STRING_LITERAL (NOTES ':' STRING_LITERAL)? DEDENT"

constraints.text: Grammar MUST compile successfully using ANTLR4 tooling without errors
Grammar MUST be unambiguous — zero ANTLR warnings in production
All GASD keywords are case-sensitive and MUST be UPPERCASE
Grammar MUST reject syntactically invalid files with clear error tokens
Grammar MUST support all GASD 1.1.0 constructs including Literal Types and expert natural language patterns
Grammar MUST reject VALIDATE statements without explicit AS TYPE binding
INLINE_TRACE (@trace) MUST NOT consume colons ':' to prevent swallowing structural delimiters

decisions.name: Input Argument Strategy
decisions.rationale: Users need to validate entire architecture projects at once, rather than passing a single file at a time. This mirrors standard compiler CLI behavior.

decisions.name: Directory Traversal
decisions.rationale: When a directory is provided, the tool should automatically locate all valid specification files within its hierarchy.

decisions.name: Output Formatting
decisions.chosen: Per-file status followed by aggregated summary (unless JSON mode)
decisions.rationale: Provides immediate feedback per file, but a high-level summary at the end prevents scrolling fatigue on large codebases.
alternatives: Per-file status followed by aggregated summary

decisions.name: AST Output Strategy
decisions.rationale: Users need flexibility to either inspect the AST directly or pipe it into other tools.

steps.target: "Print final validation summary across all files"

constraints.text: CLI MUST exit with code 1 if ANY parsed file contains errors
Directory traversal MUST only process files ending with .gasd extension
CLI MUST process all discovered files sequentially and not halt on the first file error
AST Extraction MUST ONLY occur for files that pass semantic validation
CLI MUST provide clear error messages for I/O failures during AST export

decisions.name: AST vs CST
decisions.rationale: The ANTLR parse tree (CST) includes syntactic noise (colons, commas, keywords). A clean AST simplifies downstream analysis and code generation.

decisions.name: AST Generation Method
decisions.rationale: A visitor walks the CST and constructs typed AST nodes, allowing full control over the output shape.

decisions.name: AST Node Identity
decisions.rationale: Each AST node carries a 'kind' field for easy pattern matching and type narrowing in TypeScript.

decisions.name: AST Serialization Format
decisions.rationale: JSON is the industry standard for structured data exchange and is easily consumable by downstream tooling.

constraints.text: INLINE_TRACE (@trace) MUST NOT contain a colon ':' to avoid swallowing structural delimiters.
AST MUST be deterministic: same input always produces identical AST
AST MUST enforce parent-child hierarchy (e.g. Fields under Types, Methods under Components)
Every AST node MUST include source location metadata (line, column)
AST node classes MUST represent all GASD constructs

decisions.name: Error Output Format
decisions.chosen: Dual Format: Human-Readable Console + Machine-Readable JSON
decisions.rationale: Developers need clear console output; tooling (IDEs, CI) needs structured JSON for programmatic consumption.

decisions.name: Error Severity Model
decisions.rationale: ERROR blocks compilation, WARNING flags potential issues, INFO provides advisory notes.

steps.target: "Append expected tokens or suggestions if available"

constraints.text: Syntax errors MUST show file path, line number, and column
Semantic errors MUST include context of the offending construct
Error output MUST be available in machine-readable JSON format
Error format MUST remain stable across versions for tooling integration
